target.name: Helios
target.founder: Jonathan Geifman, Elad Geffen, Guy Amir, John Hausner, Ohad Hallak

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Helios is a Series A climate and energy technology company that has raised $26M to date. The broader Climate & Energy sector includes one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers spanning fusion, battery recycling, solid-state battery, critical minerals, and green hydrogen subsectors. However, a sufficiently narrow peer set at the same stage and valuation tier does not exist to support a reliable median comparable analysis—only zero same-stage and zero adjacent-stage peers met minimum thresholds.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology provides the primary valuation signal. Using total capital raised as a value proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $52M. This approach treats the $26M raised as a floor and assumes standard venture-stage progression dynamics.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $44M to $60M, with a median of $52M. This range reflects the single applicable method and incorporates modest variance bounds around the point estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer-stage comparables, which limits cross-validation, and dependence on the 2.0x multiplier assumption—actual outcomes will depend on Helios's revenue traction, capital efficiency, and the specific terms negotiated with Series A investors. Additional data on portfolio company burn rates or revenue would sharpen the analysis materially.
